questions.prompt: When should you pick tROAS over tCPA?

choices.text: When you have accurate conversion value tracking and varied AOV. tROAS bids more aggressively on auctions predicted to drive high-value conversions and less on low-value ones — perfect for e-commerce where buying a $50 product is a different signal than buying a $500 product. tCPA treats every conversion equally regardless of value, so it's right when conversion value is roughly flat (lead-gen with uniform lead value, app installs). Requires 50+ value-attributed conversions/30 days.

questions.explanation: tROAS = varied AOV with value tracking. tCPA = flat conversion value.

questions.prompt: Brand-new campaign, 8 conversions in last 30 days. Setting tCPA at historical $40. Performance erratic. Why?

choices.text: Below the signal threshold. Smart Bidding (especially tCPA) needs roughly 30+ conversions/30 days to learn reliably; with 8 conversions, the algorithm has too little data to predict per-auction conversion probability, so bids are noisy. Switch to Maximize Conversions or Maximize Clicks to accumulate data faster, then move to tCPA once you have 30+ conversions and a defensible target. Forcing tCPA on thin signal produces exactly the erratic pattern you're seeing.

questions.explanation: Below 30 conversions/30 days, switch to Max Conversions until you have signal.
